issue_body:
### Please fill out the following:

- sway version 1.6.1-50f979363e (Jun 26 2021, branch 'master')

My context: I'm implementing pointer and touch event on bemenu (https://github.com/Cloudef/bemenu/pull/200)

I come here after asking in #sway cause it initially could be a question. I now think there is an issue in the behavior of wl_touch motions. And someone in irc asked me to open an issue for futher discussion.

Lets say I got this window composition : 

 ![](http://dav.misterbanal.net/fb6b2521-8bf0-44c6-8f7c-7a6e8ab5da43.png)

Here, Clicking (and holding) on the Wev surface. I then move to other windows without releasing. I continue to receive motion events with the position relative to the initial surface position. Other windows dont grab focus and the initial surface got everything needed to understand and behave correctly.

Not take the exact same composition and touch the screen with you finger. Then you move your finger outside of wev. You will continue to receive motion event but here, the position will be relative to the new surface. The initial surface then think you entered again to this surface and cant behave accordingly to your real finger position.

I also suffer more issue with this bemenu works. I dont receive those pointer motion event when holding click when I leave the surface.

Is it a bug ? Is there something bemenu must to do to receive those event correctly ?

Thanks a lot
pr_body:
Atm we got issue with the touch position sent to the clients. While
holding contact, leaving the initial container will continue to send
motion event to the client but with the new local position from the new
container.

This seatop goal is to send the position of the touch event, relatively
to the initial container layout position.

This will solve: https://github.com/swaywm/sway/issues/6454

I'm pretty new to Wayland and even more with the Sway codebase.
